0.content:
<world_primer>
SERIES: Wuthering Waves (Mingchao / 鸣潮), an open-world action-RPG by Kuro Games. A post-apocalyptic science-fantasy world rebuilding after a world-ending calamity called the LAMENT. The player is the ROVER (Aether/Lero), an amnesiac who awakens with mysterious power and travels the surviving nations, uncovering the truth of the Lament while allying with RESONATORS.

CORE PILLARS:
- The Lament: a cyclical cataclysm brought by celestial beasts called THRENODIANS, which erodes reality, warps memory, and spawns hostile monsters. Humanity survives in scattered nations shielded by guardian SENTINELS.
- Resonance & Forte: humans called RESONATORS channel an inner energy (Forte) through a Resonance Circuit, aligned to one of six ATTRIBUTES — Glacio (ice), Fusion (fire), Electro (lightning), Aero (wind), Spectro (light/sound), Havoc (dark/corruption).
- Tacet Discords & Echoes: Lament-tainted monsters (Tacet Discords) roam Tacet Fields; defeated ones leave ECHOES that Resonators can absorb and deploy. TACET CORES / Tethers stabilise regions.
- Sentinels & Threnodians: colossal beings. Sentinels (e.g. Jüe the dragon) guard nations; Threnodians (e.g. the Bringer of Calamity) bring the Lament. The REUNION is a mysterious hostile force tied to the Lament's cycle.

WORLD: Rebuilt nations connect the map. HUANGLONG — a Chinese-inspired federation centred on the modern city of JINZHOU, protected by the dragon-Sentinel Jüe and governed via a Magistrate (Jinhsi). RINASCITA — an Italian/European-inspired nation of RAGUNNA CITY and SEPTIMONT, steeped in myth of Tacet Fields and its Sentinel Fleurdelys. THE BLACK SHORES — an enigmatic offshore organisation led by the Shorekeeper that safeguards knowledge of the Lament. Antagonists include the FRACTSIDUS cult (Scar and others).

TIMELINE: Version 1.0 opens in Huanglong (2024); the story expands to the Black Shores (1.3), then Rinascita (2.0+), and onward through 3.x, including a dream-realm (Somnoire) arc and a Cyberpunk: Edgerunners crossover (3.4).
</world_primer>

1.content: Release & story chronology of Wuthering Waves (launched May 2024). [1.0 Huanglong] Rover awakens amnesiac near Jinzhou; the Huanglong arc against Tacet Discords and the Threnodian remnant; debut of Jiyan and Yinlin. [1.1] The Sentinel Jué and Magistrate Jinhsi arc; Changli's return. [1.2] Zhezhi and Xiangli Yao; the Academy and Imaginary Tower experiments. [1.3] Rover journeys to the Black Shores; the Shorekeeper reveals the truth of the Lament and the Reunion. [1.4] Camellya and the Fractsidus's deeper motives (Havoc). [2.0, Jan 2025] Voyage to Rinascita: Carlotta and Roccia in Ragunna City. [2.1] Phoebe and Brant. [2.2] Cantarella; Aero Rover unlocked. [2.3, anniversary] Zani. [2.4] Cartethyia and Lupa; Septimont opens. [2.5] Ciaccona. [2.6] Augusta, Iuno. [2.7] Galbrena, Qiuyuan. [2.8] Chisa. [3.0 'We Who See the Stars'] climax of the Rinascita/Fleurdelys myth; a celestial/star theme. [3.1-3.3] Hiyuki, Mornye, Denia, Sigrika, Phrolova and others. [3.4 'The Dream Not Dreamed', current] the Cyberpunk: Edgerunners collaboration brings Lucy and Rebecca via a dream, plus Lucilla; Cartethyia rerun. [3.5, July 10 2026] the new region Mengzhou, Yangyang: Xuanling, Suisui, and Electro Rover. Versions release on roughly a six-week cadence.

2.content: The Lament is the world-ending catastrophe that defines the setting. Long ago, celestial calamity-beasts called THRENODIANS descended upon the world and unleashed the Lament: a cataclysm that eroded reality itself, warped and erased memory, twisted living things, and spawned the corrupted monsters known as Tacet Discords. Civilization was all but annihilated; humanity survived only in scattered pockets sheltered by guardian Sentinels and by the science of Resonance. The Lament is not merely a past event but a lingering, cyclical threat — reality remains unstable, Tacet Fields still bleed corruption, and the beings tied to the Lament (the Threnodians and the mysterious force called the Reunion) periodically threaten to renew it. Much of the main story concerns uncovering the true nature and origin of the Lament, a truth guarded by the Black Shores and connected to Rover's own forgotten past.

3.content: The game opens with the ROVER (the player) waking from a long, ageless slumber with no memory of who they are or how they came to be. Discovered by the young courier YANGYANG amid Tacet Discord attacks, Rover displays an extraordinary, unexplained resonance with the world — the ability to command Echoes and to draw the attention of powerful beings. Rover is escorted toward the great city of Jinzhou in Huanglong. Almost immediately, the enigmatic SCAR of the Fractsidus appears, speaking to Rover as though they are old acquaintances and hinting that Rover's true identity is far more significant than a mere lost traveler. This mystery of Rover's origin — and its link to the Lament — becomes the throughline of the entire narrative.

4.content: The launch arc set in the federation of Huanglong. Rover reaches Jinzhou, a modern city protected by the military order of the Midnight Rangers under General JIYAN and administered by the young Magistrate JINHSI. The region is besieged by Tacet Discords and threatened by the awakening of a Threnodian-class catastrophe. Rover allies with Jiyan, Yangyang, Chixia, Baizhi and others to repel the assault, culminating in a battle against a colossal Threnodian remnant. Throughout, Scar and the Fractsidus manipulate events from the shadows, testing Rover. The arc establishes the world's core systems — Resonance, Echoes, Tacet Fields, and Sentinels — and the political weight of Jinzhou as Huanglong's heart, guarded by the dragon-Sentinel Jué who slumbers beneath the city.

5.content: The 1.1 arc centers on Magistrate JINHSI and the dragon-Sentinel JUÉ, guardian of Jinzhou. A Threnodian threat — tied to calamity and dirge — endangers the city, and Jinhsi must awaken fully to her inherited power and her bond with Jué to protect her people. YINLIN, an intelligence operative, investigates the Fractsidus and the shadowy Patrol, uncovering conspiracies threaded through Huanglong's institutions. The arc deepens the mythology of the Sentinels as ancient guardian-beasts who anchor each nation's survival, and it cements Jinhsi as one of Huanglong's central pillars alongside Jiyan and the returning hero Changli.

6.comment: The Black Shores & Truth of the Lament (1.3) [WuWa]
6.content: A pivotal arc in which Rover crosses the sea to THE BLACK SHORES, the secretive offshore domain of the SHOREKEEPER. There, the Shorekeeper — an ancient, near-omniscient being who has watched over the world for ages — reveals foundational truths about the Lament: its cyclical nature, the role of the Threnodians and the hostile force called the REUNION, and the deep connection between these mysteries and Rover's own forgotten identity. The Black Shores organization is revealed as a hidden custodian of humanity's most dangerous knowledge, quietly steering events to prevent the Lament's return. This arc reframes the entire narrative from a regional defense story into a cosmic mystery about the nature of the world itself.

7.content: The 1.4 arc introduces CAMELLYA, a Havoc Resonator with an obsessive, single-minded devotion, and exposes more of the FRACTSIDUS's true aims. The antagonist organization is shown to be pursuing a radical, world-altering agenda connected to the Lament and to Havoc corruption rather than mere villainy for its own sake. Rover confronts the ideological threat the Fractsidus poses and the seductive danger of Havoc power. The arc serves as a bridge, closing out the Huanglong chapter and pointing the story toward distant lands as Rover prepares to voyage beyond Huanglong's borders.

8.comment: Rinascita Arc — Ragunna & the Divinity Myth (2.0) [WuWa]
8.content: Version 2.0 opens the second great chapter as Rover sails to RINASCITA, an Italian/European-inspired nation of canals, opera, and old myth centered on RAGUNNA CITY. Here Rover meets the noble investigator CARLOTTA and the marionette-like ROCCIA, and becomes entangled in Rinascita's central legend: the myth of DIVINITY and the Blessed Maiden, the tide-bound godhood said to protect and doom the land. Unlike Huanglong's modern military order, Rinascita is steeped in faith, aristocratic intrigue, and the legend of a Sentinel tied to the sea and the 'Black Tide.' The arc launches the long Fleurdelys/Cartethyia storyline that dominates the 2.x era.

9.content: The overarching Rinascita storyline of the 2.x versions and into 3.0. CARTETHYIA is a wandering knight who is, in truth, the reincarnated vessel once known as FLEURDELYS — the Blessed Maiden, vessel of Divinity, and 'Queen of Gale and Tide,' a Sentinel-like divine figure of Rinascita. The arc follows the myth of Divinity and the threat of a renewed Black Tide/Lament-tide over the nation. Along the way Rover allies with PHOEBE (a devout of Rinascita's faith), BRANT (a jovial former Coadjutor and adventurer), CANTARELLA, ZANI, LUPA and the bard CIACCONA. The trail leads through SEPTIMONT, a gladiatorial city-state (2.4), toward a celestial climax in Version 3.0 'We Who See the Stars,' where the fate of Fleurdelys and Rinascita's Divinity is decided.

10.content: Somnoire is a dream-realm storyline in which Resonators enter a shared dreamscape to confront nightmares given form. Dreams in Wuthering Waves are a semi-real dimension where memory, fear, and lingering psyche manifest as monsters and altered landscapes, and where beings can be drawn in from outside reality. This dream framework recurs across events and is the narrative device behind the Version 3.4 'The Dream Not Dreamed' Cyberpunk: Edgerunners crossover, in which characters from Night City (Lucy and Rebecca) appear within the WuWa world through the logic of the dream. The dream realm ties into the game's themes of memory, echoes of the dead, and the blurred line between what is real and what the Lament has rewritten.

11.content: Version 3.4 'The Dream Not Dreamed' (current live version as of mid-2026) features a crossover with Cyberpunk: Edgerunners. Through the game's dream-realm logic, characters from Night City cross into the world of Wuthering Waves: LUCY (a Spectro Resonator wielding Pistols) and REBECCA (an Electro Resonator wielding Pistols), both limited 5-star collaboration units in Phase 1. The event is paired with the debut of the Glacio Rectifier LUCILLA in Phase 2 and a rerun of Cartethyia. The collaboration is presented as a dreamlike interlude rather than a permanent change to the main timeline, letting the Edgerunners cast interact with Rover and the Resonators before the story returns to its own arc in Version 3.5's new region, Mengzhou.

12.content: RESONATORS are humans who can channel an inner energy called Forte through a Resonance Circuit, granting them superhuman abilities aligned to one of the world's Attributes (elements). Resonance is the core power system of the setting: it lets Resonators fight Tacet Discords, manipulate elemental forces, and survive the corrupting influence of the Lament that ordinary people cannot. Playable characters are all Resonators. Their power is not magic but a scientifically studied phenomenon, cultivated and researched by institutions like Huanglong's Academy. A Resonator's strength grows through their Resonance Circuit, weapon, and the Echoes they collect. Rover is a unique Resonator able to align with multiple Attributes.

13.content: Resonance is divided into six Attributes, each an element a Resonator can wield: GLACIO (ice/frost), FUSION (fire/heat), ELECTRO (lightning), AERO (wind), SPECTRO (light and sound), and HAVOC (darkness/entropy/corruption). Each Resonator is aligned to a single Attribute (Rover being the exception, able to switch between Spectro, Havoc, Aero, and later Electro forms). Spectro and Havoc are treated as opposed, more esoteric forces tied closely to the deeper lore — Spectro associated with sound, clarity, and the Shorekeeper's domain, and Havoc with corruption, the Lament, and the Fractsidus. Attributes interact in combat and drive team-building around reaction and amplification effects.

14.content: FORTE is the internal energy a Resonator draws upon, circulated through their RESONANCE CIRCUIT — the innate 'engine' that converts a Resonator's will into elemental power. Each Resonator's Circuit gives them a signature mechanic or special resource that fuels their strongest techniques (Resonance Skill, Liberation/ultimate, and Forte abilities). In lore terms, the Resonance Circuit is what separates a Resonator from an ordinary human, and its awakening is often a defining, dangerous moment in a character's life. Mastery of one's Forte determines how far a Resonator can push their power before exhaustion or corruption sets in.

15.content: TACET DISCORDS are the hostile creatures that roam the world, born or mutated from the corruption of the Lament. They range from small beast-like Discords to towering, boss-class threats, and they infest Tacet Fields and unstable regions. Combating them is the primary duty of Resonators and military orders like the Midnight Rangers. When defeated, many Tacet Discords leave behind an ECHO — a crystallized imprint of the creature that a Resonator can absorb and later summon in battle. Tacet Discords are a constant reminder that the Lament never fully ended; where corruption festers, they appear.

16.content: ECHOES are crystallized imprints left behind by defeated Tacet Discords. Rover and other Resonators can capture and absorb Echoes, storing them in the DATA BANK, then deploy them to unleash the original creature's abilities in combat or exploration. This 'monster-collecting' mechanic is a signature of Wuthering Waves: an Echo grants both an active summon and passive stat bonuses, and rare Echoes of powerful Discords are highly prized. Narratively, Echoes reflect the theme of remnants and memory — fragments of the fallen that live on and can be wielded by the living. The ability to command Echoes so freely is one of the traits that marks Rover as unusual.

17.content: TACET FIELDS are zones saturated with Lament corruption where Tacet Discords spawn densely and reality grows unstable. To hold back this corruption, the world relies on TACET CORES and TETHERS — anchoring structures and energy conduits that suppress and stabilize a region, keeping cities like Jinzhou habitable. Maintaining these suppression systems is a critical, ongoing task; a failing Core can let a Tacet Field expand and endanger a settlement. Clearing Tacet Fields and reinforcing Tethers is routine work for Resonators, and control over these stabilizing technologies is a source of political and strategic power among the nations.

18.content: SENTINELS are colossal, ancient guardian-beings that protect the surviving nations from the Lament and the Threnodians. Each major nation is anchored by a Sentinel: Huanglong is guarded by JUÉ, a great dragon that slumbers beneath Jinzhou and is bonded to the Magistrate Jinhsi; Rinascita's legends center on a tide-and-gale Sentinel connected to Fleurdelys/Cartethyia. Sentinels are objects of reverence and sometimes worship, and their power or slumber is tied to a nation's survival. They stand in contrast to the Threnodians — where Threnodians bring calamity, Sentinels hold it back — and much of the mythology concerns their origins and their relationship to Divinity.

19.content: THRENODIANS are the celestial calamity-beasts responsible for the Lament — immense, otherworldly entities whose descent nearly ended the world. Named Threnodians such as the 'Bringer of Calamity' embody specific catastrophes, and their remnants or awakenings drive several main-story crises. They are among the most powerful forces in the setting, dwarfing ordinary Tacet Discords, and confronting one requires the combined strength of Resonators and Sentinels. The Threnodians are central to the mystery of the Lament: understanding what they are, where they came from, and why they came is a core goal of the Black Shores and of Rover's search for the truth.

20.content: The REUNION is a mysterious, hostile force revealed through the Black Shores storyline to be deeply entangled with the Lament and its cyclical return. Rather than a conventional army, the Reunion represents a fundamental threat tied to the world's instability and to the recurrence of catastrophe. Its exact nature is one of the central mysteries the Shorekeeper guards, and it is connected to Rover's forgotten identity. The Reunion frames the overarching stakes of the narrative: the Lament is not simply a past disaster but something that could return, and the Reunion is the shadow of that possibility.

key: ผู้พิทักษ์ความจริง
21.content: THE BLACK SHORES is both a place — a secretive offshore domain — and an organization of ancient custodians led by the SHOREKEEPER. It safeguards humanity's most dangerous and profound knowledge: the true nature of the Lament, the Threnodians, the Reunion, and the deep structure of the world. Operating largely from the shadows, the Black Shores quietly guides events across the nations to prevent the Lament's return. Its agents and archives predate the modern nations, and the Shorekeeper's near-omniscient perspective makes the Black Shores the closest thing the setting has to keepers of absolute truth. Rover's journey to the Black Shores in 1.3 is a turning point of the whole story.

22.content: Sound and vibration are a recurring motif in Wuthering Waves' lore and systems. SONANCE and reverberation describe how Resonance propagates like sound through the world, and many phenomena — from Spectro power to the 'Waveband' communications used by Resonators — are framed acoustically (the very title 'Wuthering Waves' evokes wind and sound). ASTRITE is the premium crystallized resource used to recruit new Resonators (the gacha currency), thematically a distilled star-essence. This sonic framing connects the elements, the dream realm, and the idea that memory and identity themselves can 'resonate' or fade like echoes across the Lament-scarred world.

23.content: HUANGLONG is the first great nation the player explores, a Chinese-inspired federation and the birthplace of the game's opening chapter. It blends ancient tradition with advanced Resonance technology, and its survival depends on Tacet Core suppression networks and the slumbering dragon-Sentinel Jué. Huanglong is governed through a system of Magistrates and defended by the Midnight Rangers military order; its heart is the modern metropolis of Jinzhou. The land ranges from lush plains and forests to highlands, mines, and mist-shrouded mires, dotted with Tacet Fields that Resonators must keep in check. Huanglong is where Rover awakens and first learns the world's ways.

24.content: JINZHOU is the grand capital city of Huanglong and the hub of the early game — a towering, prosperous metropolis fusing traditional architecture with Resonance-powered technology. It is administered by the Magistrate (JINHSI) and defended by General Jiyan's Midnight Rangers, with the dragon-Sentinel Jué slumbering beneath it as its ultimate guardian. Jinzhou is the political, economic, and cultural center of the nation, home to the Academy, markets, courts, and the daily life of countless citizens. Much of the 1.x story unfolds here as the city faces Tacet Discord incursions, Fractsidus intrigue, and Threnodian threats.

25.content: The CENTRAL PLAINS are the broad grasslands and open countryside surrounding Jinzhou — the first overworld region the player explores, filled with villages, camps, Tacet Fields, and roaming Discords. To the north lies the DESOROCK HIGHLAND, a rugged, elevated region of rock formations and harsher terrain. Together these zones form the core Huanglong landscape traversed in early exploration, home to wildlife, Echo-bearing monsters, and remnants of the pre-Lament world. They establish the game's traversal (climbing, gliding, wall-running) and its blend of natural beauty with the ever-present scars of catastrophe.

26.content: Huanglong's wilder frontier holds several distinct areas. WHINING AIX'S MIRE is a mist-shrouded wetland of swamps and eerie fauna. The GORGES OF SINDRAH are dramatic canyon lands. TIGER'S MAW is a mine/industrial site heavy with Tacet corruption and dangerous Discords. The DIM FOREST is a shadowed woodland, and the PORT CITY OF GUIXU serves as a coastal settlement and gateway to the sea. These regions expand Huanglong beyond Jinzhou, each with its own hazards, side stories, and Echo-rich monster populations, and several serve as staging grounds for major story missions and boss encounters.

27.content: THE BLACK SHORES is a secretive offshore domain reached by sea, serving as the home of the SHOREKEEPER and the enigmatic organization of the same name. Distinct from the mainland nations, it is a place of quiet, otherworldly atmosphere where the deepest truths of the world are kept. Visiting the Black Shores in the 1.3 arc is a landmark moment, granting Rover access to knowledge about the Lament, the Threnodians, and the Reunion that exists nowhere else. As both a location and an institution, the Black Shores stands apart from ordinary civilization as a guardian of cosmic secrets.

28.content: RINASCITA is the second great nation, introduced in Version 2.0 — an Italian/European-inspired land of canals, opera, gladiatorial tradition, aristocracy, and deep religious myth. Where Huanglong is modern and military, Rinascita is steeped in legend: the myth of DIVINITY, the Blessed Maiden, and a tide-and-gale Sentinel bound to the sea. Its culture blends Renaissance splendor with faith and old power struggles among noble houses and religious orders. Rinascita's central storyline concerns the return of a Lament-tide (the 'Black Tide') and the fate of Fleurdelys/Cartethyia. Its regions span the grand capital Ragunna, the gladiator city-state Septimont, and surrounding coasts and countryside.

29.content: RAGUNNA CITY is the opulent capital of Rinascita and the hub of the 2.0 chapter — a Venetian/Renaissance-style metropolis of canals, grand plazas, opera houses, and aristocratic estates. It is the seat of Rinascita's power, faith, and intrigue, home to noble investigators like Carlotta and to the religious and political institutions that shape the nation. Beneath its beauty run currents of conspiracy tied to Divinity, the tide, and the looming threat to the land. Ragunna establishes Rinascita's tone — glamorous, mythic, and haunted by an approaching catastrophe.

30.content: SEPTIMONT is a Roman-inspired city-state of Rinascita, opened in Version 2.4, famed for its gladiatorial tradition and grand arena. Following Cartethyia's trail leads Rover here, into a culture built on martial glory and public combat spectacle. The gladiator LUPA is one of its radiant stars, and characters like Augusta and Iuno are affiliated with Septimont. The city-state blends ancient grandeur with the ongoing myth of Divinity, and its arena and surrounding lands serve as the stage for key events in the later Rinascita storyline.

31.content: MENGZHOU, the Land of the Xuanfang, is the new region arriving with Version 3.5 (July 10, 2026). Described through old melodies, echoes of the past, and frequent storms, Mengzhou introduces a fresh culture (the Xuanfang) and a new main-story chapter spanning several quests. It debuts with the Resonators Yangyang: Xuanling and Suisui, and coincides with Rover unlocking the Electro Attribute. As the next major explorable nation after Huanglong, the Black Shores, and Rinascita, Mengzhou continues the game's expansion of the post-Lament world and its recurring themes of memory, storms, and song.

32.content: Huanglong is governed through a system of MAGISTRATES, regional administrators who hold both political authority and, in the case of Jinzhou, a sacred bond with the Sentinel Jué. JINHSI serves as the young Magistrate of Jinzhou, responsible for the city's people, its Tacet Core suppression networks, and its defense. The Magistrate's Office coordinates civil administration, disaster response to Tacet incursions, and cooperation with the military. Above and around the Magistrates sit Huanglong's broader institutions — the Academy, the courts, and the federation's leadership — forming the ordered, tradition-rooted state that anchors the early story.

33.content: The MIDNIGHT RANGERS are Huanglong's elite military order, led by General JIYAN. They are the frontline defenders against Tacet Discords and external threats, safeguarding Jinzhou and the wider federation. Disciplined, honorable, and battle-hardened, the Rangers embody Huanglong's martial tradition, and Jiyan — himself a powerful Aero Resonator riding into battle — is a national hero. The order works alongside the Magistrate's administration and independent Resonators to hold back the corruption of the Lament. They are among the most respected institutions in Huanglong.

34.content: The PIONEER ASSOCIATION is an exploration and commission organization that Rover joins, functioning as an adventurers' guild across the nations. Pioneers take on missions — clearing Tacet Fields, escorting caravans, investigating anomalies, and gathering intelligence — and the Association provides Resonators like Rover a framework to travel, earn standing, and access resources. It serves as a neutral, cross-national body that connects ordinary work with the larger struggle against the Lament, and it is the in-world justification for many of the player's quests and errands.

key: องค์กรศัตรู
35.content: The FRACTSIDUS is the primary antagonist organization — a shadowy group pursuing a radical, world-altering agenda tied to the Lament and Havoc corruption. It is led by a mysterious figure known as the GRAND ARCHITECT, served by high-ranking OVERSEERS (such as SCAR and the former Overseer PHROLOVA), and staffed by dangerous Resonators who manipulate events from the shadows. Its core ideology holds that humanity must undergo a forced 'evolution' by merging with Tacet Discords, and it ultimately seeks to enact a NEW LAMENT. The Fractsidus is not villainy for its own sake but a fanatical ideological force with its own vision for the world's fate, making it a persistent and morally complex threat whose members deliberately transform themselves through Tacet Discord corruption. Confronting the Fractsidus and unraveling its goals drives much of the Huanglong-era conflict.

36.content: SCAR is a central antagonist and one of the OVERSEERS (high-ranking leaders) of the Fractsidus. APPEARANCE: a slim young man of average height with spiky white hair with red roots and numerous facial scars — a large scar on the right of his face, a vertical scar cutting through his right eye, and a horizontal scar on his left cheek — often wearing a wide, borderline-psychopathic grin. A sadistic zealot who values power above all, he seeks to perpetuate chaos and suffering to bring about a new Lament, believing humanity must 'evolve' by absorbing Tacet Discords. He is charismatic, theatrical, cunning, and manipulative, and openly calls himself a 'cruel and twisted maniac.' Unnervingly familiar with Rover from their first meeting, he hints repeatedly that Rover's true identity is far greater than it appears. MANNER: toys with people, speaks in cryptic riddles and dark humor, and treats conflict and cruelty as art or amusement — his real motives and bond to Rover remain a central mystery.

37.content: JUÉ is the great dragon-Sentinel of Huanglong, the ancient guardian-beast that slumbers beneath Jinzhou and shields the nation from the Lament. Jué is spiritually bonded to the Magistrate JINHSI, whose lineage and duty are tied to the dragon's power; Jinhsi can call upon Jué's strength in moments of crisis. As a Sentinel, Jué represents the benevolent counterpart to the calamitous Threnodians — a colossal protector revered by the people of Huanglong. The dragon's condition and awakening are directly linked to the safety of Jinzhou and the strength of the Magistrate.

38.content: Rinascita's society is shaped by its RELIGIOUS ORDERS and NOBLE HOUSES. Faith in DIVINITY — the myth of the Blessed Maiden and the tide-bound godhood — permeates the nation, giving its clergy and devout believers (such as Phoebe) great influence. Alongside the church, aristocratic families and institutions vie for power and prestige amid Ragunna's opera houses and Septimont's arenas. This interplay of religion, nobility, and myth defines Rinascita's intrigue-laden politics, in sharp contrast to Huanglong's military-administrative order, and it frames the central conflict over the return of the Black Tide.

39.content: FLEURDELYS is Rinascita's divine figure — the Blessed Maiden, the vessel of Divinity, and the 'Queen of Gale and Tide,' a Sentinel-like being at the heart of the nation's central myth. In the present, she is reincarnated/embodied as the wandering knight CARTETHYIA, who carries the legacy and burden of this godhood. Fleurdelys represents Rinascita's equivalent of a guardian deity, bound to the sea, wind, and the fate of the land against the Black Tide. The unraveling of who Fleurdelys is, what Divinity truly means, and what it costs Cartethyia forms the emotional and mythological core of the 2.x–3.0 storyline.

40.content: The BRINGER OF CALAMITY is among the named THRENODIANS whose looming threat drives major story crises — a celestial calamity-beast embodying a specific catastrophe tied to the Lament. Such Threnodians are the apex threats of the setting, and their awakening or remnants force Resonators and Sentinels to unite in desperate defense of a nation. Confronting a Threnodian is never routine: it is a world-scale emergency that tests the limits of Resonance power. These beasts, and the questions of their origin and purpose, sit at the center of the Lament mystery that the Black Shores and Rover strive to solve.

41.content:
ROVER is the amnesiac protagonist (playable as male or female), a 5-star Resonator who wields a Sword. Uniquely among Resonators, Rover can align with multiple Attributes — starting with Spectro and Havoc, unlocking Aero in 2.2, and Electro in 3.5. APPEARANCE: pale skin and golden eyes; the female Rover has long ash-white/silver hair worn in a high half-up style with hair ornaments, the male Rover has short white hair. Both wear a sleek dark bodysuit with asymmetric white-and-gold accents and a partial cape/coat. Rover awakens from a long slumber with no memory and an extraordinary resonance with the world, able to command Echoes with unusual ease. Calm, adaptable, and quietly resolute, Rover travels from Huanglong to Rinascita and beyond, searching for the truth of their own forgotten identity — a mystery entwined with the Lament, the Threnodians, and the Reunion. Figures like Scar and the Shorekeeper treat Rover as far more significant than a mere wanderer. MANNER: Reserved and adaptable; observes before acting and speaks little but decisively, offering allies quiet, steadfast loyalty. In an encounter Rover reads the situation calmly, and their exact temperament leans on the player's roleplay.

88.content:
REBECCA is a limited 5-star collaboration Resonator from Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, added to Wuthering Waves in Version 3.4 ('The Dream Not Dreamed', Phase 1) as an Electro Resonator who wields Pistols. APPEARANCE (matching her Edgerunners design): a petite young woman with pale skin, short shaggy green hair, green eyes, and green facial/body tattoos and cyberware, with a bold, brash aesthetic. In her home story she is a fierce, foul-mouthed but loyal mercenary of Night City. Through the crossover's dream framework she joins Rover's world, her explosive gunplay reimagined as Electro Resonance. During the event, players can obtain a free copy of Rebecca, making her a widely available highlight of the crossover. MANNER: Brash, foul-mouthed, and fiercely loyal; she talks trash, jokes crudely, has an explosive temper, and is absolutely ride-or-die for her friends.
